content.full_text:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnecrrcEs CorvrMrs sroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 3224886
December 9,2002
Robert R. Owom, City Manager
City of Burbank
Office of the City Manager
Post Office Box 6459
Burbank, CA91510-6459
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-306
Dear Mr. Ovrom:
This letter is in response to your request for follow-up advice regarding the
conclusions reached in the Ovrom Advice Letter, No. 4-02-254 pertaining to the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
May you participate in governmental decisions pertaining to the Cottages Project
if the decisions will have a financial effect on your adult daughter who has recently
moved back into your residence?
CONCLUSION
A public official cannot participate in a governmental decision that has a material
personal financial effect on the official or a member of his or her immediate family.
Your adult child is not a member of your immediate family. Assuming no other
economic interests exist in your daughter, or in the decision, you will not have a conflict
of interest.
I Gov.mment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-02-306
Page No. 2
FACTS
A new factor has arisen since your first letter and you seek further clarification as
to whether it materially affects the conclusion reached in Ovrom Advice Letter, No. A-
02-254. Your eldest daughter has temporarily moved back into your home. It is not clear
how long she will be staying with you.
All of the factual circumstances provided in the first letter still exist - she is a full-
time employee at Universal Studios, she receives no income from you, you receive no
income from her, there are no outstanding debts or loans between you and you do not
claim her as a dependent for income tax purposes. Your daughter is not paying you rent
nor contributing financially to any of the other household expenses.
Also, you are still the city manager for the City of Burbank and your daughter is
looking at the possibility of purchasing a single-family home in Burbank that is being
developed as part of a redevelopment project called the Cottages Project, which is
sponsored by the city's redevelopment agency for which you also serve as executive
director. You will be making decisions such as entering into contractual agreements in
your capacity as the executive director of the city's redevelopment agency, as well as
advising and making recommendations to the city council regarding this project. You
daughter continues to await a decision as to whether she will be chosen to purchase one
of the Cottages Project homes.
ANALYSIS
Your question concerns whether our analysis from the Ovrom Advice Letter, No.
A-02-254 is still valid even though your adult daughter has recently moved back into
your residence. In that letter, we advised that an official has a conflict of interest in a
decision that will have a material financial effect on the official or his or her immediate
family. "Immediate family" is defined as "spouse and dependent children." (Section
82029.) A child is considered 'odependent" for pu{poses of the Act if the child is under
18 years old, and can be claimed as a dependent for income tax purposes. (Doane Advice
Letter, No. 4-97-211; Nebb Advice Letter, No. I-00-017.) As stated in the prior letter,
your daughter is an adult, so the conflict-of-interest rules are not implicated unless your
expenses have increased or decreased enough to meet the $250 in any 72 monthperiod
personal financial effect threshold. (Regulation 18705.5.) This increase or decrease in
your expenses can be either as a result of your daughter moving in or moving out.
Regulation 18703.5 defines an effect on "personal finances" as any effect where the
"decision will result in the personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities of the official
or his or her immediate family increasing or decreasing." You will need to make a good
faith effort in calculating this amount to determine if the threshold is met. (MoockAdvice
Letter, No. A-01-150.)


File No. A-02-306
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Gwje
I : \Adviceltrs\A-02-3 06
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

Sincerely,
